ksa_text: Knowledge of model monitoring requirements, including data drift detection, prediction drift, performance degradation metrics, input data distribution monitoring, latency and throughput tracking, alerting thresholds, and retraining triggers appropriate for different model types and serving patterns.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of data visualization tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of data visualization tools and techniques
Knowledge of test procedures, principles, and methodologies (e.g., Capabilities and Maturity Model Integration (CMMI)).
Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: AI/ML Engineering falls outside the primary competency scope of the Domain Data Lead. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is AI/ML Engineering truly out-of-scope for the Domain Data Lead? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list AI/ML Engineering as a core or recommended competency for the Domain Data Lead. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms AI/ML Engineering falls outside the established competency profile for the Domain Data Lead. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: AI/ML Engineering is outside the competency scope of the Domain Data Lead. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
